postData.contentHtml:
The world of mixed martial arts erupted as Ronda Rousey, the trailblazing former UFC champion, stepped back into the octagon for the first time in ten years. In a highly anticipated double comeback bout against fellow pioneer Gina Carano, Rousey proved she hadn't missed a beat, securing a breathtaking victory via her signature armbar submission in just 17 seconds.

Broadcast live on Netflix, the event marked a significant moment for both fighters and the sport. Rousey, known for her dominant first-round finishes, wasted no time, immediately taking Carano down and expertly transitioning into the armbar that made her a global superstar. The swift and decisive victory left no doubt about her enduring prowess.

Carano, returning after an even longer absence, was quickly overwhelmed by Rousey's relentless attack. Despite the quick finish, the event generated considerable buzz, with news outlets widely reporting on Rousey's spectacular return and the definitive nature of her performance. Social media sentiment was largely positive, with fans celebrating 'Rowdy' Ronda's dominant comeback, though some online debate was noted regarding the brevity of the fight after such a long buildup.

This comeback fight not only reminded fans of Rousey's undeniable skill but also highlighted the continued evolution and mainstream appeal of MMA, with major streaming platforms like Netflix now hosting live events.
postData.title: Ronda Rousey's Explosive Comeback: 17-Second Armbar Stuns Gina Carano in Highly Anticipated Bout
postData.summary: MMA icon Ronda Rousey made a triumphant return to the cage after a decade-long hiatus, delivering a stunning 17-second armbar submission victory over Gina Carano in their much-hyped double comeback fight, broadcast live on Netflix.

sources.title: Ronda Rousey only needs 17 seconds to defeat Gina Carano in stunning end to anticipated fight
Ronda Rousey Demolishes Gina Carano In 17 Seconds And Retires
Ronda Rousey needs just 17 seconds to tap out Gina Carano with signature armbar submission
